signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.4706 — G10 scorecard leader NZD (+0.664%) vs laggard CHF (-0.812%) creates a 1.476% spread, the widest in the G10 complex. Despite the 250bp NZD/CHF carry advantage, the pair faces immediate pressure as WTI Oil hits $85 on Strait of Hormuz tensions. CHF safe-haven demand typically compresses this carry spread during energy-driven risk-off shocks. NZD COT LF net short -27,675 (18% long) indicates extreme bearish positioning, leaving the pair vulnerable to a squeeze if geopolitical tensions de-escalate before the RBNZ meeting.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.33 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but institutional positioning signals exhaustion. COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) is crowded, while the 25d RR at -1.58 shows a strong put skew as the market pays for downside protection. VRP at +3.7% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.5%) confirms elevated fear of a sharp reversal. Price is holding above 160.00, a level previously associated with BoJ intervention risk. Carry dominates until VIX > 25, but the options skew suggests a local top is forming.

signals.title: USD/CAD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Oil Shock
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4146 — Institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. COT LF net short -87,846 (22.6% long) shows specs are heavily short, while retail is 78% short, fading the recent move. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has broken to -0.900 (z=-2.02σ), meaning the pair is no longer tracking the crude rally. This decoupling suggests the 150bp USD carry advantage and US data outperformance (CESI +65) are overriding the CAD's traditional oil link. Watch the BoC Monetary Policy Report at 10:45 for a shift in the 2.25% policy stance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6922 — Options market pricing extreme protection with a VRP of +8.5% (IV 15.3% vs HV30 6.8%). While the 60bp AUD carry advantage supports the pair, the Economic Surprise Index (CESI) shows a strong negative bias for AUD (-10) vs a strong positive for USD (+65). This data divergence suggests the AUD's recent resilience is at risk if US CPI (due today) surprises to the upside. Moderate put skew (25d RR -0.92) confirms the market is leaning toward AUD downside protection.
